tagline: Estimate welding rod usage per joint length
keywords: welding rod, electrode consumption, welding estimate
calcFn: var deposition = rodLength * 0.6; var rodsNeeded = Math.ceil((jointLength * passes) / deposition); var totalWeight = rodsNeeded * 0.12; return {rodsNeeded: fmt(rodsNeeded,0), totalWeight: fmt(totalWeight,1)};

faq.q: How do I estimate rod consumption?
faq.a: Divide total weld length by the deposition length per rod (typically 60% of rod length).

faq.q: What affects rod usage?
faq.a: Joint type, rod diameter, number of passes, and welder technique all impact consumption.

formulas.name: Rods Needed
formulas.explanation: Each rod deposits about 60% of its length as weld metal.

tagline: Calculate hydraulic cylinder push and pull force
keywords: hydraulic cylinder, force, PSI, bore size
calcFn: var boreArea = Math.PI * Math.pow(bore/2, 2); var rodArea = Math.PI * Math.pow(rodDia/2, 2); var pushForce = boreArea * pressure; var pullForce = (boreArea - rodArea) * pressure; return {pushForce: fmt(pushForce,0), pullForce: fmt(pullForce,0)};

faq.q: How is cylinder force calculated?
faq.a: Force equals bore area times system pressure. Pull force subtracts the rod area.

faq.q: What PSI is typical?
faq.a: Most industrial hydraulic systems run 2000-5000 PSI.

formulas.name: Push Force
formulas.explanation: Push force uses the full bore area times pressure.

tagline: Calculate conveyor belt speed and throughput
keywords: conveyor belt, speed, throughput, RPM
calcFn: var outputRpm = rpm / ratio; var circumference = Math.PI * rollerDia / 12; var beltSpeed = outputRpm * circumference; var beltSpeedMph = beltSpeed * 60 / 5280; return {beltSpeed: fmt(beltSpeed,1), beltSpeedMph: fmt(beltSpeedMph,2)};

faq.q: How is belt speed calculated?
faq.a: Belt speed = roller circumference × output RPM after gear reduction.

faq.q: What is a typical conveyor speed?
faq.a: Most material handling conveyors run 50-200 ft/min.

formulas.name: Belt Speed
formulas.formula: Speed = π × Roller Dia × (Motor RPM / Gear Ratio)
formulas.explanation: Output RPM times roller circumference gives linear belt speed.

tagline: Calculate the operating cost of compressed air systems
keywords: compressed air, compressor cost, CFM, electricity
calcFn: var kw = hp * 0.746; var monthlyKwh = kw * hours; var monthlyCost = monthlyKwh * rate; var annualCost = monthlyCost * 12; return {monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How much does compressed air cost?
faq.a: Electricity typically accounts for 76% of a compressor's lifecycle cost.

faq.q: How to reduce compressed air costs?
faq.a: Fix leaks, reduce pressure to minimum needed, and use variable speed drives.

formulas.name: Monthly Cost
formulas.explanation: Convert HP to kW, multiply by hours and rate.

tagline: Estimate industrial coating material needs
keywords: industrial paint, coating, coverage rate, square feet
calcFn: var gallons = Math.ceil((area * coats) / coverage); var totalCost = gallons * priceGal; return {gallons: fmt(gallons,0), totalCost: dollar(totalCost)};

faq.q: What is typical industrial paint coverage?
faq.a: Most industrial coatings cover 300-400 sq ft per gallon at recommended mil thickness.

faq.q: Should I add waste factor?
faq.a: Add 10-20% for overspray and waste depending on application method.

formulas.name: Gallons Needed
formulas.explanation: Total area to be painted divided by per-gallon coverage.

tagline: Calculate torque wrench settings with extensions
keywords: torque wrench, torque setting, extension, adapter
calcFn: var effectiveLength = wrenchLength + extLength; var dialSetting = targetTorque * (wrenchLength / effectiveLength); var appliedForce = targetTorque * 12 / effectiveLength; return {dialSetting: fmt(dialSetting,1), appliedForce: fmt(appliedForce,1)};

faq.q: Do extensions change torque readings?
faq.a: Yes, adding a crow foot or extension changes the effective lever arm, requiring a corrected dial setting.

faq.q: When do I need to adjust?
faq.a: Only when the extension adds length at an angle to the torque wrench handle.

formulas.name: Corrected Torque
formulas.explanation: Adjust the dial to compensate for the increased lever arm.

tagline: Calculate your GPA from course grades and credits
keywords: GPA calculator, grade point average, college GPA
calcFn: var totalCredits = creditsA + creditsB + creditsC + creditsD + creditsF; var points = creditsA*4 + creditsB*3 + creditsC*2 + creditsD*1 + creditsF*0; var gpa = totalCredits > 0 ? points / totalCredits : 0; return {gpa: fmt(gpa,2), totalCredits: fmt(totalCredits,0)};

faq.q: How is GPA calculated?
faq.a: Multiply each grade's point value by credits, sum them, then divide by total credits.

faq.q: What GPA do I need for honors?
faq.a: Most schools require 3.5+ for cum laude, 3.7+ for magna cum laude, 3.9+ for summa cum laude.

formulas.name: GPA
formulas.formula: GPA = Σ(Grade Points × Credits) / Total Credits
formulas.explanation: Weighted average of grade points by credit hours.

tagline: Estimate your student loan payoff timeline and total interest
keywords: student loan, repayment, payoff, interest
calcFn: var monthlyRate = rate / 100 / 12; var months = Math.ceil(-Math.log(1 - (balance * monthlyRate / payment)) / Math.log(1 + monthlyRate)); var totalPaid = months * payment; var totalInterest = totalPaid - balance; return {months: fmt(months,0), totalInterest: dollar(totalInterest), totalPaid: dollar(totalPaid)};

faq.q: How long will it take to pay off student loans?
faq.a: Depends on balance, rate, and payment. The standard plan is 10 years (120 months).

faq.q: Should I pay extra?
faq.a: Even small extra payments significantly reduce total interest and payoff time.

formulas.name: Months to Payoff
formulas.formula: n = -ln(1 - Balance × r / Payment) / ln(1 + r)
formulas.explanation: Solve the amortization formula for the number of payments.

tagline: Project your 529 college savings plan growth
keywords: 529 plan, college savings, education fund
calcFn: var r = returnRate / 100 / 12; var n = years * 12; var fvLump = current * Math.pow(1 + r, n); var fvMonthly = monthly * ((Math.pow(1 + r, n) - 1) / r); var futureValue = fvLump + fvMonthly; var totalContributions = current + (monthly * n); var earnings = futureValue - totalContributions; return {futureValue: dollar(futureValue), totalContributions: dollar(totalContributions), earnings: dollar(earnings)};

faq.q: What is a 529 plan?
faq.a: A tax-advantaged savings plan for education expenses. Earnings grow tax-free when used for qualified expenses.

faq.q: How much should I save?
faq.a: Aim to cover at least 50-75% of projected college costs through savings.

formulas.name: Future Value
formulas.explanation: Compound growth of lump sum plus regular contributions.

tagline: Calculate the total value of a scholarship over your degree
keywords: scholarship, financial aid, college cost, tuition
calcFn: var totalValue = annual * years; var coveragePct = Math.min((annual / tuition) * 100, 100); var remaining = Math.max(tuition - annual, 0); return {totalValue: dollar(totalValue), coveragePct: fmt(coveragePct,1), remaining: dollar(remaining)};

faq.q: Do scholarships count as income?
faq.a: Scholarships used for tuition and fees are generally tax-free. Room and board portions may be taxable.

faq.q: Can I stack multiple scholarships?
faq.a: Yes, but total aid usually cannot exceed cost of attendance.

formulas.name: Total Value
formulas.explanation: Simple multiplication of annual award by years of coverage.

tagline: Calculate fair tutoring rates and projected earnings
keywords: tutoring rate, tutor pricing, hourly rate
calcFn: var baseRates = {'Elementary': 25, 'Middle School': 30, 'High School': 40, 'College': 50, 'Graduate': 65}; var base = baseRates[subject] || 35; var hourlyRate = base + (experience * 3); var weeklyIncome = hourlyRate * hoursWeek; var monthlyIncome = weeklyIncome * 4.33; return {hourlyRate: dollar(hourlyRate), weeklyIncome: dollar(weeklyIncome), monthlyIncome: dollar(monthlyIncome)};

faq.q: What do tutors typically charge?
faq.a: Rates range from $25/hr for elementary to $65+/hr for graduate-level tutoring.

faq.q: How does experience affect rates?
faq.a: Each year of experience typically justifies a $2-5/hr increase.

formulas.name: Hourly Rate
formulas.explanation: Start with subject base rate and add experience premium.

tagline: Analyze the impact of class size on educational outcomes
keywords: class size, student teacher ratio, education quality
calcFn: var ratio = students / teachers; var effectiveRatio = students / (teachers + aides * 0.5); var rating = effectiveRatio <= 12 ? 'Excellent' : effectiveRatio <= 18 ? 'Good' : effectiveRatio <= 24 ? 'Average' : 'Needs Improvement'; return {ratio: fmt(ratio,1), effectiveRatio: fmt(effectiveRatio,1), rating: rating};

faq.q: What is the ideal class size?
faq.a: Research suggests 15-18 students per teacher provides optimal outcomes.

faq.q: Do teaching aides help?
faq.a: Yes, each aide effectively reduces the ratio, though not as much as a full teacher.

formulas.name: Student-Teacher Ratio
formulas.explanation: Simple division of total students by number of teachers.

tagline: Calculate soil volume needed for raised garden beds
keywords: raised bed, soil volume, garden soil, cubic feet
calcFn: var cubicFeet = length * width * (depth / 12); var cubicYards = cubicFeet / 27; var bags = Math.ceil(cubicFeet / 2); return {cubicFeet: fmt(cubicFeet,1), cubicYards: fmt(cubicYards,2), bags: fmt(bags,0)};

faq.q: How deep should a raised bed be?
faq.a: At least 6 inches for most vegetables, 12+ inches for root crops and better drainage.

faq.q: What soil mix is best?
faq.a: A common mix is 60% topsoil, 30% compost, and 10% perlite or vermiculite.

formulas.name: Volume
formulas.explanation: Length times width times depth converted to feet.

tagline: Design your drip irrigation system flow rates
keywords: drip irrigation, GPH, flow rate, emitter
calcFn: var totalFlow = emitters * gph; var waterUsed = totalFlow * (runtime / 60); var monthlyWater = waterUsed * 30; return {totalFlow: fmt(totalFlow,1), waterUsed: fmt(waterUsed,1), monthlyWater: fmt(monthlyWater,0)};

faq.q: What flow rate should I use?
faq.a: 0.5-1 GPH for most plants, 2-4 GPH for trees and large shrubs.

faq.q: How long should I run drip irrigation?
faq.a: 20-45 minutes for most gardens, depending on soil type and plant needs.

formulas.name: Total Flow
formulas.explanation: Sum of all emitter flow rates gives total system demand.

tagline: Calculate optimal plant spacing and total plants needed
keywords: plant spacing, garden layout, plants per row
calcFn: var spacingFt = spacing / 12; var rows = Math.floor(bedWidth / spacingFt); var plantsPerRow = Math.floor(bedLength / spacingFt); var totalPlants = rows * plantsPerRow; return {rows: fmt(rows,0), plantsPerRow: fmt(plantsPerRow,0), totalPlants: fmt(totalPlants,0)};

faq.q: Why does spacing matter?
faq.a: Proper spacing ensures adequate sunlight, airflow, and nutrient access for each plant.

faq.q: Can I plant closer in raised beds?
faq.a: Yes, raised beds with good soil can often use spacing 25% tighter than ground planting.

formulas.name: Total Plants
formulas.explanation: Grid layout based on spacing in both directions.

tagline: Balance your compost pile's carbon to nitrogen ratio
keywords: compost, carbon nitrogen ratio, C:N ratio, composting
calcFn: var totalCarbon = (brownLbs * brownCN) + (greenLbs * greenCN); var totalNitrogen = brownLbs + greenLbs; var blendRatio = totalCarbon / totalNitrogen; var rating = blendRatio >= 25 && blendRatio <= 35 ? 'Ideal' : blendRatio < 25 ? 'Too much nitrogen - add browns' : 'Too much carbon - add greens'; return {blendRatio: fmt(blendRatio,1), rating: rating};

faq.q: What is the ideal C:N ratio?
faq.a: The ideal range is 25:1 to 35:1 for active composting.

faq.q: What are browns and greens?
faq.a: Browns are carbon-rich (leaves, cardboard, straw). Greens are nitrogen-rich (food scraps, grass clippings).

formulas.name: Blend C:N
formulas.formula: C:N = (Brown lbs × Brown C:N + Green lbs × Green C:N) / (Brown lbs + Green lbs)
formulas.explanation: Weighted average of carbon-to-nitrogen ratios.

tagline: Calculate grass seed quantity for overseeding your lawn
keywords: overseeding, grass seed, lawn care, seeding rate
calcFn: var rates = {'Kentucky Bluegrass': 2, 'Perennial Ryegrass': 4, 'Tall Fescue': 6, 'Bermuda': 1.5, 'Zoysia': 1.5}; var multipliers = {'Light Overseeding': 0.5, 'Moderate Repair': 1, 'Heavy Renovation': 1.5}; var base = rates[seedType] || 3; var mult = multipliers[condition] || 1; var seedLbs = (area / 1000) * base * mult; var bags = Math.ceil(seedLbs / 5); var cost = bags * 25; return {seedLbs: fmt(seedLbs,1), bags: fmt(bags,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: When is the best time to overseed?
faq.a: Fall is ideal for cool-season grasses, late spring for warm-season varieties.

faq.q: Should I aerate before overseeding?
faq.a: Yes, core aeration improves seed-to-soil contact significantly.

formulas.name: Seed Amount
formulas.explanation: Base rate per 1000 sq ft adjusted for lawn condition.

tagline: Calculate grow light wattage and coverage for indoor plants
keywords: grow light, indoor garden, PAR, wattage coverage
calcFn: var wpf = {'Leafy Greens': 20, 'Herbs': 25, 'Vegetables': 30, 'Flowering/Fruiting': 40, 'Seedlings': 15}; var eff = {'LED': 1, 'HPS': 1.5, 'Fluorescent': 2, 'CMH': 1.3}; var base = wpf[plantType] || 30; var mult = eff[lightType] || 1; var watts = area * base * mult; var monthlyCost = (watts / 1000) * 18 * 30 * 0.12; return {watts: fmt(watts,0), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.q: How many watts per square foot?
faq.a: LED: 20-40W/sq ft depending on plant type. HPS needs 50% more wattage.

faq.q: How high should grow lights be?
faq.a: Typically 12-24 inches above plants for LED, 24-36 inches for HPS.

formulas.name: Watts
formulas.formula: Watts = Area × W/sqft × Light Type Efficiency
formulas.explanation: Required wattage based on plant needs and light technology efficiency.

tagline: Estimate your vehicle's value over time
keywords: car depreciation, vehicle value, car worth, resale value
calcFn: var depRates = [0.20, 0.15, 0.13, 0.12, 0.11, 0.10, 0.09, 0.08, 0.07, 0.06]; var value = purchasePrice; for (var i = 0; i < Math.min(age, 10); i++) { value *= (1 - (depRates[i] || 0.05)); } var mileAdj = 1 - ((mileage - 12000) * 0.000005 * age); value *= Math.max(mileAdj, 0.5); var totalLoss = purchasePrice - value; var annualLoss = age > 0 ? totalLoss / age : 0; return {currentValue: dollar(value), totalLoss: dollar(totalLoss), annualLoss: dollar(annualLoss)};

faq.q: How fast do cars depreciate?
faq.a: New cars lose about 20% in year one, then 10-15% per year after.

faq.q: What cars hold value best?
faq.a: Trucks, SUVs, and luxury brands like Toyota and Porsche depreciate slowest.

formulas.name: Depreciation
formulas.explanation: Compound depreciation applied year over year.

tagline: Calculate your actual cost per mile for fuel
keywords: fuel cost, cost per mile, gas mileage, MPG
calcFn: var costPerMile = gasPrice / mpg; var annualFuel = annualMiles * costPerMile; var monthlyFuel = annualFuel / 12; return {costPerMile: fmt(costPerMile * 100, 1), annualFuel: dollar(annualFuel), monthlyFuel: dollar(monthlyFuel)};

faq.q: What is the average cost per mile?
faq.a: At $3.50/gal and 28 MPG, that's about 12.5 cents per mile for fuel alone.

faq.q: What about total cost per mile?
faq.a: Including insurance, maintenance, and depreciation, the IRS rate is 67 cents/mile (2024).

formulas.name: Cost per Mile
formulas.explanation: Divide fuel price by miles per gallon.

tagline: Compare tire sizes and see the impact on speedometer accuracy
keywords: tire size, tire comparison, speedometer error, tire diameter
calcFn: var origSidewall = origWidth * origAspect / 100 / 25.4; var origDia = (origRim + 2 * origSidewall); var newSidewall = newWidth * newAspect / 100 / 25.4; var newDia = (newRim + 2 * newSidewall); var speedoError = ((newDia - origDia) / origDia) * 100; return {origDia: fmt(origDia,1), newDia: fmt(newDia,1), speedoError: fmt(speedoError,1)};

faq.q: How much tire size difference is acceptable?
faq.a: Stay within 3% of the original diameter to avoid speedometer and handling issues.

faq.q: Does tire size affect gas mileage?
faq.a: Larger/wider tires can reduce MPG by 1-3% due to increased rolling resistance.

formulas.name: Tire Diameter
formulas.explanation: Rim diameter plus twice the sidewall height.

tagline: Estimate when your brake pads need replacement
keywords: brake pad, brake life, brake wear, replacement
calcFn: var wearRates = {'Highway (light braking)': 0.00008, 'Mixed': 0.00012, 'City (heavy braking)': 0.00020}; var wearPerMile = wearRates[driving] || 0.00012; var remaining = thickness - minThickness; var milesLeft = remaining / wearPerMile; var monthsLeft = milesLeft / monthlyMiles; return {milesLeft: fmt(milesLeft,0), monthsLeft: fmt(monthsLeft,0)};

faq.q: How long do brake pads last?
faq.a: Typically 25,000-65,000 miles depending on driving habits and pad material.

faq.q: What is the minimum safe thickness?
faq.a: Most manufacturers recommend replacement at 3mm or when the wear indicator is exposed.

formulas.name: Miles Remaining
formulas.formula: Miles = (Current - Min Thickness) / Wear Rate per Mile
formulas.explanation: Remaining pad material divided by per-mile wear rate.

tagline: See how much you can save by refinancing your auto loan
keywords: car refinance, auto loan, refinance savings, interest rate
calcFn: var cr = currentRate/100/12; var nr = newRate/100/12; var currentPayment = balance * cr / (1 - Math.pow(1+cr, -currentMonths)); var newPayment = balance * nr / (1 - Math.pow(1+nr, -newMonths)); var currentTotal = currentPayment * currentMonths; var newTotal = newPayment * newMonths; var totalSavings = currentTotal - newTotal; var monthlySavings = currentPayment - newPayment; return {currentPayment: dollar(currentPayment), newPayment: dollar(newPayment), monthlySavings: dollar(monthlySavings), totalSavings: dollar(totalSavings)};

faq.q: When should I refinance my car?
faq.a: When you can get a rate at least 1-2% lower than your current rate.

faq.q: Are there refinance fees?
faq.a: Some lenders charge $0-$75. Factor this into your savings calculation.

formulas.name: Monthly Payment
formulas.explanation: Standard amortization formula for fixed-rate loans.

tagline: Determine your optimal oil change schedule
keywords: oil change, maintenance schedule, synthetic oil, oil life
calcFn: var intervals = {'Conventional': 5000, 'Synthetic Blend': 7500, 'Full Synthetic': 10000}; var severeMulti = {'Normal': 1, 'Severe (towing, dusty, short trips)': 0.7, 'Extreme (racing, off-road)': 0.5}; var costs = {'Conventional': 40, 'Synthetic Blend': 55, 'Full Synthetic': 75}; var intervalMiles = intervals[oilType] * (severeMulti[conditions] || 1); var intervalMonths = intervalMiles / monthlyMiles; var changesPerYear = 12 / intervalMonths; var annualCost = changesPerYear * (costs[oilType] || 50); return {intervalMiles: fmt(intervalMiles,0), intervalMonths: fmt(intervalMonths,1), changesPerYear: fmt(changesPerYear,1), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How often should I change oil?
faq.a: Every 5,000 miles for conventional, 7,500-10,000 for synthetic under normal conditions.

faq.q: Does synthetic oil last longer?
faq.a: Yes, full synthetic typically lasts twice as long as conventional oil.

formulas.name: Interval
formulas.explanation: Base interval adjusted for driving severity.

tagline: Calculate how much life insurance coverage you need
keywords: life insurance, coverage amount, insurance needs, DIME method
calcFn: var incomeReplace = income * yearsReplace; var totalNeed = incomeReplace + debt + education; var gap = Math.max(totalNeed - existing, 0); return {totalNeed: dollar(totalNeed), gap: dollar(gap)};

faq.q: How much life insurance do I need?
faq.a: The DIME method: Debt + Income replacement + Mortgage + Education costs.

faq.q: Term vs whole life?
faq.a: Term is cheaper and covers most needs. Whole life includes investment but costs 5-10x more.

formulas.name: DIME Method
formulas.explanation: Sum of income replacement, debts, and education funding needs.

tagline: Estimate your ACA marketplace health insurance premium
keywords: health insurance, ACA premium, marketplace, subsidy
calcFn: var basePremiums = {'Bronze': 350, 'Silver': 450, 'Gold': 550, 'Platinum': 700}; var base = basePremiums[tier] || 450; var ageFactor = 1 + ((age - 21) * 0.015); var famFactor = 1 + ((familySize - 1) * 0.6); var monthlyPremium = base * ageFactor * famFactor; var fpl = 15060 + (familySize - 1) * 5380; var fplPct = income / fpl * 100; var subsidy = fplPct < 400 ? monthlyPremium * 0.3 : 0; monthlyPremium = monthlyPremium - subsidy; var annualPremium = monthlyPremium * 12; return {monthlyPremium: dollar(monthlyPremium), annualPremium: dollar(annualPremium), subsidy: dollar(subsidy)};

faq.q: Do I qualify for a subsidy?
faq.a: If your income is below 400% of the federal poverty level, you may qualify for premium tax credits.

faq.q: What plan tier should I choose?
faq.a: Bronze for low premiums/high deductibles, Silver for subsidies, Gold/Platinum for frequent care.

formulas.name: Premium
formulas.formula: Premium = Base × Age Factor × Family Size Factor - Subsidy
formulas.explanation: Adjusted base premium minus any applicable subsidies.

tagline: Find the optimal deductible to minimize total insurance costs
keywords: auto insurance, deductible, car insurance, optimal deductible
calcFn: var savings = premium500 - premium1000; var deductibleDiff = 500; var breakeven = savings > 0 ? Math.ceil(deductibleDiff / savings) : 0; var fiveYearSavings = (savings * 60) - (claimFreq * deductibleDiff); var recommendation = fiveYearSavings > 0 ? 'Higher deductible saves ' + dollar(fiveYearSavings) + ' over 5 years' : 'Lower deductible is better with your claim frequency'; return {savings: dollar(savings), breakeven: fmt(breakeven,0), recommendation: recommendation};

faq.q: Should I raise my deductible?
faq.a: If you can afford the higher out-of-pocket cost and rarely file claims, a higher deductible usually saves money.

faq.q: How much does raising the deductible save?
faq.a: Going from $500 to $1000 typically saves 15-30% on comprehensive and collision premiums.

formulas.name: Breakeven
formulas.explanation: How many months of premium savings to recoup the higher deductible.

tagline: Determine if you need umbrella insurance and how much
keywords: umbrella insurance, liability, excess coverage
calcFn: var riskMulti = {'Low (no pool/dogs)': 1, 'Medium (pool or dog)': 1.5, 'High (teen driver, rental property)': 2}; var multi = riskMulti[riskLevel] || 1; var baseNeed = Math.max(netWorth - autoLimit, 0); var recommended = Math.ceil(baseNeed * multi / 1000000) * 1000000; recommended = Math.max(recommended, 1000000); var estimatedCost = 200 + ((recommended / 1000000 - 1) * 100); return {recommended: dollar(recommended), estimatedCost: dollar(estimatedCost)};

faq.q: What is umbrella insurance?
faq.a: Extra liability coverage beyond your auto and home policies, typically starting at $1 million.

faq.q: How much does umbrella insurance cost?
faq.a: Usually $150-300/year for $1 million, with each additional million costing $75-100.

formulas.name: Coverage Need
formulas.formula: Coverage = (Net Worth - Existing Limits) × Risk Multiplier
formulas.explanation: Cover your assets above existing liability limits, adjusted for risk.

tagline: Calculate how much disability insurance coverage you need
keywords: disability insurance, income protection, long-term disability
calcFn: var monthlyIncome = income / 12; var monthlyBenefit = Math.min(monthlyIncome * 0.6, expenses * 1.1); var coveragePct = (monthlyBenefit / monthlyIncome) * 100; var savingsMonths = savings / expenses; var waitingPeriod = savingsMonths >= 6 ? 180 : savingsMonths >= 3 ? 90 : 30; return {monthlyBenefit: dollar(monthlyBenefit), coveragePct: fmt(coveragePct,0), waitingPeriod: fmt(waitingPeriod,0)};

faq.q: How much disability coverage do I need?
faq.a: Aim for 60% of gross income, which approximates your after-tax take-home pay.

faq.q: What is the waiting period?
faq.a: The elimination period before benefits start. Longer waits mean lower premiums.

formulas.name: Monthly Benefit
formulas.explanation: Replace 60% of income or cover expenses plus a buffer.

tagline: Calculate when pet insurance pays for itself
keywords: pet insurance, breakeven, vet costs, pet health
calcFn: var annualPremium = monthlyPremium * 12; var eligible = Math.max(expectedVetBills - deductible, 0); var reimbursed = eligible * (reimbursement / 100); var netSavings = reimbursed - annualPremium; return {annualPremium: dollar(annualPremium), reimbursed: dollar(reimbursed), netSavings: dollar(netSavings)};

faq.q: Is pet insurance worth it?
faq.a: It depends on your pet's health risks. Breeds prone to health issues benefit most.

faq.q: When does pet insurance break even?
faq.a: Typically when annual vet bills exceed premiums plus deductible.

formulas.name: Net Value
formulas.formula: Net = (Vet Bills - Deductible) × Reimbursement% - Annual Premium
formulas.explanation: Reimbursement amount minus what you pay in premiums.

tagline: Calculate fuel costs for your RV road trip
keywords: RV fuel cost, road trip, RV MPG, gas cost
calcFn: var gallons = distance / mpg; var fuelCost = gallons * fuelPrice; var costPerMile = (fuelPrice / mpg) * 100; return {gallons: fmt(gallons,1), fuelCost: dollar(fuelCost), costPerMile: fmt(costPerMile,1)};

faq.q: What MPG do RVs get?
faq.a: Class A: 6-10 MPG, Class C: 10-14 MPG, Travel trailers reduce tow vehicle MPG by 30-50%.

faq.q: Diesel vs gas RV?
faq.a: Diesel RVs often get 1-3 MPG better but diesel fuel can cost more per gallon.

formulas.name: Fuel Cost
formulas.explanation: Distance divided by fuel economy times price per gallon.

tagline: Calculate your RV's remaining payload capacity
keywords: RV payload, GVWR, cargo capacity, weight limit
calcFn: var ccc = gvwr - dryWeight; var passengerWeight = passengers * 175; var waterWeight = waterGallons * 8.34; var remaining = ccc - passengerWeight - waterWeight; return {ccc: fmt(ccc,0), remaining: fmt(remaining,0)};

faq.q: What is CCC?
faq.a: Cargo Carrying Capacity = GVWR minus the dry weight of the RV.

faq.q: How much do water and passengers weigh?
faq.a: Water weighs 8.34 lbs/gallon. Passengers average 175 lbs each per RVIA standard.

formulas.name: Remaining Payload
formulas.formula: Remaining = GVWR - Dry Weight - Passengers - Water
formulas.explanation: Subtract fixed loads from total cargo capacity.

tagline: Size a solar panel system for your RV
keywords: RV solar, solar panel, boondocking, off-grid RV
calcFn: var dailyWh = dailyKwh * 1000; var efficiency = 0.75; var totalWatts = Math.ceil(dailyWh / (sunHours * efficiency)); var panels = Math.ceil(totalWatts / panelWatts); var batteryAh = Math.ceil((dailyWh * 2) / 12 / 0.5); return {totalWatts: fmt(totalWatts,0), panels: fmt(panels,0), batteryAh: fmt(batteryAh,0)};

faq.q: How many solar panels for an RV?
faq.a: Most RVs need 400-800W for moderate use (lights, fridge, charging).

faq.q: Why 2x battery capacity?
faq.a: Lithium batteries should only discharge to 20%, lead-acid to 50%, so double daily use.

formulas.name: Total Watts
formulas.explanation: Account for 25% system losses from wiring, controller, and inverter.

tagline: Budget your RV campground costs for a trip
keywords: RV campground, camping cost, RV park, travel budget
calcFn: var privateCost = privateNights * 55; var publicCost = publicNights * 30; var totalCost = privateCost + publicCost; var avgPerNight = nights > 0 ? totalCost / nights : 0; return {totalCost: dollar(totalCost), avgPerNight: dollar(avgPerNight)};

faq.q: How much do RV campgrounds cost?
faq.a: Private parks average $40-70/night, public parks $20-40, and free BLM/dispersed camping is $0.

faq.q: How to reduce campground costs?
faq.a: Mix free camping with paid parks, use memberships like Thousand Trails or Passport America.

formulas.name: Total Cost
formulas.explanation: Average rates for private and public campgrounds.

tagline: Estimate your RV's value and depreciation over time
keywords: RV depreciation, RV value, motorhome value, resale
calcFn: var annualRates = {'Class A Motorhome': 0.12, 'Class C Motorhome': 0.11, 'Fifth Wheel': 0.10, 'Travel Trailer': 0.09, 'Pop-up/Tent Trailer': 0.08}; var rate = annualRates[rvType] || 0.10; var firstYearHit = age >= 1 ? 0.20 : 0; var value = purchasePrice * (1 - firstYearHit); for (var i = 1; i < age; i++) { value *= (1 - rate); } if (age === 0) value = purchasePrice; var depreciation = purchasePrice - value; var depPct = (depreciation / purchasePrice) * 100; return {currentValue: dollar(value), depreciation: dollar(depreciation), depPct: fmt(depPct,1)};

faq.q: How fast do RVs depreciate?
faq.a: RVs lose 15-20% in year one, then 8-12% annually after that.

faq.q: Do RVs hold value?
faq.a: Towable RVs (travel trailers, fifth wheels) hold value better than motorhomes.

formulas.name: RV Value
formulas.explanation: 20% first-year hit followed by steady annual depreciation.

tagline: Calculate the right generator size for your RV
keywords: RV generator, generator watts, generator sizing, portable generator
calcFn: var acWatts = {'None': 0, '13,500 BTU': 1500, '15,000 BTU': 2000}; var acStart = {'None': 0, '13,500 BTU': 3000, '15,000 BTU': 3500}; var mwWatts = microwave === 'Yes (1000W)' ? 1000 : 0; var fridgeWatts = fridge === 'Yes' ? 150 : 0; var fridgeStart = fridge === 'Yes' ? 600 : 0; var running = (acWatts[ac]||0) + mwWatts + fridgeWatts + extras; var starting = (acStart[ac]||0) + mwWatts + fridgeStart + extras; var recommended = Math.ceil(starting / 500) * 500; return {runningWatts: fmt(running,0), startingWatts: fmt(starting,0), recommended: fmt(recommended,0)};

faq.q: What size generator for an RV?
faq.a: 2000-3000W for small RVs, 3500-5500W for most Class C/A with AC.

faq.q: Inverter vs conventional generator?
faq.a: Inverter generators are quieter and produce cleaner power for electronics.

formulas.name: Generator Size
formulas.formula: Size = Σ Starting Watts of all appliances
formulas.explanation: Sum all appliance starting watts and round up to next generator size.
